goal: First-lecture 5-minute 'hello robot' demo that 20 undergrads can reproduce on mixed laptops.
thoughts: Pedagogy: one demo, numbered steps, 'step does X because Y'. Hardware check first — integrated GPUs fail silently on RT-heavy templates. Franka-cube is the canonical hello-world: small, state-based, forgiving. Cloud fallback for weakest machines. Reproducibility beats flash.
code:
# Step 0: hardware gate — names who needs cloud fallback
hardware_compatibility_check()

# Step 1: show students what starter scenes exist (pedagogic catalog)
scene_aware_starter_prompts()
list_scene_templates()

# Step 2: the simplest viable demo — Franka + cube, state-based, no cameras
quick_demo(demo_name="<FRANKA_CUBE_STARTER>")

# Step 3: pedagogic narration
# "We loaded <TEMPLATE>. The Franka is a 7-DOF arm — step Play (space) to
#  see gravity; the cube falls. Step 2 because PhysX needs one tick to apply mass."

# Integrated-GPU fallback: same demo on cloud session — identical prim paths,
# so students' notes still work.
failure_modes: Picking a camera-heavy demo — OOMs integrated GPUs
No hardware gate — half the class freezes on launch
Improvisation during live demo — students cannot reproduce at home
Skipping the 'press space to play' step — scene looks dead
